data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $238 million yesterday, with Fidelity FBTC leading the way with a net inflow of $108 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $238 million yesterday (November 21, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net inflow of $108 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.798 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF BTC, with a net inflow of $84.9339 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.951 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net outflow of $122 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $62.703 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $110.11 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.53%, and a cumulative net inflow of $57.635 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $55.7113 million yesterday, reversing eight consecutive days of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (November 21, Eastern Time), Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $55.7113 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net inflow of $95.3989 million, bringing FETH's historical total net inflow to $2.542 billion. This was followed by the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a net inflow of $7.7347 million, bringing ETH's historical total net inflow to $1.424 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $53.6827 million, bringing ETHA's historical total net inflow to $12.89 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $16.859 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.1%, and a cumulative net inflow of $12.632 billion.

data.title: Robert Kiyosaki Sells Bitcoin for Significant Profit, Plans Future Investments
data.description: According to PANews, Robert Kiyosaki, the author of 'Rich Dad Poor Dad,' announced on the X platform that he sold Bitcoin valued at $2.25 million for approximately $90,000 each. These Bitcoins were originally purchased a few years ago at $6,000 per coin. Kiyosaki used the proceeds to acquire two surgical centers and invest in a billboard company. Despite the sale, he remains optimistic about Bitcoin and intends to increase his holdings in the future.

data.title: Cardano experienced a chain fork due to an incorrect order format, but no user funds were lost.
data.description: A blockchain forked into two chains on Friday due to a malformed proxy transaction triggering a software vulnerability. The transaction passed verification on newer nodes but was rejected by older software versions, causing the network to split. Cardano's ecosystem governance organization, Intersect, stated in its incident report that the "toxic transaction" exploited a vulnerability in the underlying software library, splitting the network into a "poisoned chain" containing the transaction and a "healthy chain" not containing it. Co-founder Charles Hoskinson initially claimed it was a "premeditated attack," but a user named Homer J. later publicly admitted responsibility, stating that he was negligent in attempting to reproduce the "toxic transaction" and relied on AI-generated instructions. The user stated that he had no malicious intent and did not gain any financial benefit from it. Intersect confirmed that no users suffered financial losses, and most retail wallets were unaffected. The price of the ADA token fell by more than 6% as a result of the incident. (Decrypt)

data.title: Satoshi Nakamoto's assets have decreased by $47 billion from their all-time high, representing a loss of 34%.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, (as the price of BTC has plummeted) Satoshi Nakamoto's address has lost $47 billion, reducing his personal wealth to $90.7 billion, the lowest point since reaching an all-time high of $137 billion last October. The $47 billion loss resulting from the market downturn represents approximately 34% of his all-time high net worth.

data.title: The US government transferred assets worth $7.5 million this morning, including NEXO, WETH, and TRX.
data.description: Arkham officially announced on the X platform that the US government transferred another $7.5 million worth of cryptocurrency this morning. In the past 24 hours, the US government transferred $300,000 in NEXO, $3 million in WETH, and $4.2 million in TRX. The NEXO and TRX were seized from Alameda between 2022 and 2024, while the WETH was seized from Bitfinex hacker Ilya Lichtenstein between the same period.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum (ETH) surpasses $2,889, the cumulative short liquidation intensity on major centralized exchanges (CEX) will reach $1.078 billion. Conversely, if ETH falls below $2,616, the cumulative long liquidation intensity on these exchanges will amount to $899 million.

data.title: Opinion: Powell may face significant internal resistance; the December rate cut meeting may see multiple dissenting votes.
data.description: Nick Timiraos, a vocal critic of the Federal Reserve, writes that Trump stated this week that he expects interest rates to fall sharply after a new Fed chairman is appointed next May. However, growing opposition within the Fed to a December rate cut suggests his wish may be difficult to fulfill. Whether Powell chooses to hold rates steady or cut them in December, he faces the most significant internal resistance in his nearly eight-year term. This division could extend into next year, meaning that even a change of chairman does not guarantee further rate cuts. Some worry that if Trump fails to achieve his goal, he might resort to more aggressive measures to undermine the central bank's independence in exchange for rate cuts. For over 30 years, Fed chairs have sought the broadest possible consensus on interest rate decisions, with no decision passed by a narrow majority. But the December meeting is highly likely to see three or more dissenting votes. Evercore ISI economist Krishna Guha stated, "We are witnessing a breakdown in the decision-making process, and next year we may see a serious split within the committee. (December) feels like a preview of 2026." This suggests an unprecedented prospect: monetary policy outcomes may be decided by a very rare, narrow majority (rather than the long-standing tradition of pursuing broad consensus), and Trump's new chairperson may not be able to control the situation every time. (Jinshi)

data.title: Aave founder: ETHLend will be relaunched in 2026
data.description: Aave founder and CEO Stani stated on the X platform: "The Bitcoin collateral is real Bitcoin, unencased, and I guarantee the relaunch of ETHLend in 2026." ETHLend is an independent lending application and the predecessor to Aave, which evolved into a decentralized peer-to-peer lending marketplace after being rebranded as ETHLend in 2018.

data.title: Federal Reserve Faces Internal Resistance Amid Trump's Rate Cut Expectations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump expressed this week that he anticipates a significant drop in interest rates following the appointment of a new Federal Reserve Chair in May next year. However, there is growing opposition within the Federal Reserve against a rate cut in December, suggesting that Trump's expectations may not be realized.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell faces substantial internal resistance, the most severe in nearly eight years, whether he decides to maintain the current rates or proceed with a rate cut in December. This division could persist into the next year, indicating that a change in leadership does not guarantee further rate cuts. Some are concerned that if Trump does not achieve his desired outcome, he might take more aggressive measures to undermine the central bank's independence to secure rate cuts.

For over 30 years, Federal Reserve Chairs have sought to achieve the broadest possible consensus on interest rate decisions, avoiding decisions passed by a narrow majority. However, the December meeting is likely to see three or more dissenting votes.

Krishna Guha, an economist at Evercore ISI, noted that the decision-making process is breaking down, and next year could witness a deeply divided committee. The December meeting feels like a preview of 2026, suggesting an unprecedented scenario where monetary policy outcomes might be decided by a rare narrow majority, rather than the traditional broad consensus. Trump's appointed new chair may not always be able to control the situation.

data.title: Trader Eugene posted an article "Surrendering to the Market": Licking his wounds and waiting to see what happens next.
data.description: According to @ai_9684xtpa, trader Eugene posted on his personal channel, "I acted too early; the current market is hellishly difficult. I'm licking my wounds and will observe again." This seems to be an admission of a previous trading decision error. Previously, he had publicly stated that he had increased his holdings of ETH and SOL and was bullish on UNI in the short term. Now that UNI has fallen below the $6.5 support level, his previous arguments have become invalid.

data.title: Investor Reconsiders Strategy Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description: According to Foresight News, a user known as @ai_9684xtpa has reported that an investor, referred to as 'Pigeon' in a Telegram group, admitted to acting prematurely in the market. The investor hinted at reassessing their position, suggesting they might be dealing with losses or holding onto investments. Previously, Pigeon had publicly announced an increase in holdings of ETH and SOL, expressing short-term optimism for UNI. However, UNI has since fallen below the $6.5 threshold, undermining their earlier market predictions.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Acquires Significant Holdings Amid Debt Concerns
data.description: According to Foresight News, blockchain monitoring service Lookonchain has reported that a major Ethereum investor, known as 66kETHBorrow, has purchased 114,684 ETH over the past two days, valued at approximately $319 million. This acquisition brings the investor's total holdings to 489,696 ETH, worth around $1.35 billion. The address is currently facing a debt of $648 million on the Aave platform, with an ETH liquidation price set at approximately $1,594.

data.title: Nakamoto CEO: MSCI's removal of digital asset reserve companies highlights industry discrimination and conflicts of interest; regulatory agencies should intervene and investigate.
data.description: Regarding the recent market rumors of MSCI removing digital asset companies from its index, Nakamoto CEO and Bitcoin Magazine Chairman David Bailey posted on the X platform, stating, "For a systemically important stock index, after years of actively supporting the inclusion of Bitcoin and digital assets, to single out Bitcoin and digital asset companies for de-indexing and disqualification is discriminatory and capricious. We should call it Operation Choke Point 3.0. What has changed? Why is Bitcoin being singled out while all other commodity-related industries (oil, gold, agriculture) are fully eligible for indexing? If this policy passes, it will: • Harm investors by triggering a forced sell-off of hundreds of billions of dollars in Bitcoin stocks and hundreds of billions of dollars in lost future earnings. • Cool investment in Bitcoin startups and private companies that rely on public market exits. • Prevent publicly traded companies from holding or using Bitcoin due to concerns about index eligibility. • Weaken America's leadership in a strategically important, rapidly growing global industry. I believe this policy topic was first published on October 10th." The current situation is highly questionable, and I believe it is likely to be a significant factor in our future market conditions. Banks and index providers should not have the right to exclude 40% of capital market funding from the entire industry, especially when there is direct competition within the industry. This is a clear conflict of interest and absolutely deserves scrutiny from Congress and regulators. We fought and won fair access for banks. We fought and won against the Bitcoin ETF. Now we must fight (and win) for the public's right to fair investment.

data.title: Moonrock Capital founder alleges insider trading allegations surrounding Coinbase's acquisition of Vector.fun; TNSR stock price surges 8-fold in two days before plummeting 40%.
data.description: Simon Dedic, founder of Moonrock Capital, posted on X that the Coinbase acquisition of Vector.fun showed signs of insider manipulation. He noted that TNSR surged approximately eightfold in two days while the overall market was weak, only to fall back about 40% on the day the news was announced, with no apparent attempts to conceal the activity by the relevant accounts. He believes that Vector.fun's developer, Tensor, and its token appear to remain independent of the acquisition, and infers that Tensor will cash out a large, undisclosed sum, while TNSR holders will "get nothing," further highlighting the misalignment between equity and tokens.

data.description:
